jokes.text: If Elaine Nardo doesn't care enough to tell us personally, then to hell with her.
jokes.setup: Someone announces Elaine's Europe trip as exciting news
jokes.punchline: Louie immediately turns petty and vindictive about not being told personally
jokes.explanation: Perfect Louie character moment - petty, vindictive, and immediately contradictory. Strong character integration.

jokes.text: I want you to see the Sistine Chapel just the way Michaelangelo saw it.
jokes.setup: Tony offers to show Elaine Rome in a romantic way
jokes.punchline: He wants her to see the Sistine Chapel 'just the way Michelangelo saw it' - which would be without the ceiling painted
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist humor from Tony's character - historically ignorant but delivered with complete sincerity. The long pause after suggests everyone processing the stupidity.

jokes.text: it has been our history that there has been a certain attraction between you and me here. Especially you for me.
jokes.setup: Alex explaining why he can't go to Europe with Elaine
jokes.punchline: His awkward, presumptuous claim that she's especially attracted to him
jokes.explanation: Classic Alex neurotic behavior - overthinking and making assumptions about attraction.

jokes.text: it's every decent man's fantasy that Europe is filled with steamy, sultry women just waiting to throw themselves at the feet of some American stud.
jokes.setup: Alex continues explaining his concerns about traveling with Elaine
jokes.punchline: His grandiose description of himself as 'some American stud'
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Alex's delusions of grandeur beneath his neuroses.

jokes.text: It has been the lesson of my life that nothing that ever sounds that good ever really happens.
jokes.setup: Elaine describing the perfect vacation scenario
jokes.punchline: Alex's pessimistic life philosophy delivered as universal truth
jokes.explanation: Perfect encapsulation of Alex's worldview - pessimistic but relatable wisdom.

jokes.text: Who didn't clean the puke out of cab 415? And yet...
jokes.setup: Romantic Europe planning discussion
jokes.punchline: Immediate cut to Louie's gross taxi dispatcher reality, followed by Alex's contemplative 'And yet...'
jokes.explanation: Excellent comedic juxtaposition between romantic dreams and gritty reality, with perfect timing.

jokes.text: How's the French coming along? Just fine. Answer me in French. Just fine.
jokes.setup: Question about Alex's French language skills with request for demonstration
jokes.punchline: Alex repeating 'Just fine' in English when asked to speak French
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective setup/punchline with perfect economy. Classic language learning humor.

jokes.text: Yeah, only in a sort of stunning way.
jokes.setup: Someone asking if a woman is pretty
jokes.punchline: Alex's understated 'only in a sort of stunning way'
jokes.explanation: Nice deadpan delivery typical of Alex's character - downplaying while actually emphasizing.

jokes.text: The walk over here was the longest walk of my life, but the walk back is going to be even longer if you don't smile.
jokes.setup: Alex approaching an attractive woman
jokes.punchline: His vulnerable admission that rejection will make the walk back worse
jokes.explanation: Charming and vulnerable pickup line that shows Alex's neurotic self-awareness.

jokes.text: Get lost. ... I'd be very obligated to you as a human being if you'd just let me sit next to you for just a couple of seconds.
jokes.setup: Woman clearly rejecting Alex
jokes.punchline: Alex's increasingly desperate and formal plea to sit next to her
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy showing Alex's inability to take a hint and his overly formal desperation.

jokes.text: If I have to mace you, I will. That's not mace, that's Binaca. Even Binaca, when you get it in your eye, still stings.
jokes.setup: Woman threatening Alex with what she thinks is mace
jokes.punchline: Alex correcting her that it's breath spray, then noting it still stings
jokes.explanation: Absurd escalation with Alex's matter-of-fact correction and practical observation about Binaca.

jokes.text: Alex, how'd it go? Oh, it's a little too early to tell.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking about Alex's obvious rejection
jokes.punchline: Alex's understated response suggesting it might still work out
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alex response - refusing to admit defeat with classic understatement.

jokes.text: All right, so I'm the oldest guy on the plane. Are you satisfied now?
jokes.setup: Todd corrects Alex about their age difference
jokes.punchline: Alex's defensive escalation to the extreme conclusion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Alex's insecurity about aging. The defensive anger is very Alex

jokes.text: What's the difference in our ages anyway, six or seven years? I'd say more like 12, sir. All right, so I'm the oldest guy on the plane. Are you satisfied now?
jokes.setup: Alex trying to establish he's not that much older than young Todd
jokes.punchline: Todd's honest assessment makes Alex spiral into self-pity
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Alex's insecurity about aging and tendency toward dramatic self-pity.

jokes.text: Todd has just asked us to jog to Germany.
jokes.setup: Introduction of young, energetic Todd
jokes.punchline: The absurd suggestion to jog to Germany as typical Todd behavior
jokes.explanation: Efficient character establishment through absurd suggestion that fits the young traveler stereotype.

jokes.text: We could have walked and saved the bus fare. Alex, we're in Europe. Every penny counts.
jokes.setup: Alex complaining about slow European transportation
jokes.punchline: Elaine's practical response about penny-pinching while on European vacation
jokes.explanation: Mild humor about tourist budget consciousness and different travel philosophies.

jokes.text: 'The Queen and the Duke of Edinburgh in front of Buckingham Palace.' ... 'A nice couple we met at the hotel.'
jokes.setup: Writing postcards with tourist photos
jokes.punchline: Describing the Queen and Duke as 'a nice couple we met at the hotel'
jokes.explanation: Clever visual gag about tourist photography and creative postcard writing.

jokes.text: Ah, Alex, Elaine's platonic friend.
jokes.setup: James being introduced to Alex
jokes.punchline: Immediately categorizing Alex as the 'platonic friend'
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Alex being immediately friendzoned by sophisticated rival.

jokes.text: On your date?
jokes.setup: Being invited to join Elaine and James
jokes.punchline: Alex's incredulous question about joining their romantic date
jokes.explanation: Simple cringe moment showing Alex's awkward position and social awareness.

jokes.text: Maybe even pick up a wench or two. Ooh! Break some hearts, you rogue.
jokes.setup: Alex trying to sound cavalier about his evening plans
jokes.punchline: James mockingly encouraging his 'roguish' behavior
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between Alex's forced bravado and James's condescending encouragement.

jokes.text: Alex asks 'Do you mind?' and sits down alone, followed by 'Watch this' and an apparent pratfall
jokes.setup: Alex trying to impress or connect with someone
jokes.punchline: His attempt backfires into physical comedy
jokes.explanation: Physical comedy moment that fits Alex's character - his attempts at confidence leading to embarrassment

jokes.text: [Greek taverna scene with 'Opa! Yasu!' celebration and Alex getting injured]
jokes.setup: Enthusiastic Greek celebration
jokes.punchline: Alex getting hurt during the festivities
jokes.explanation: Standard fish-out-of-water physical comedy with cultural elements.

jokes.text: Fish... and friendship. And who's contributing what? Who cares? That's the beauty of it.
jokes.setup: Elaine waxing poetic about Greek atmosphere
jokes.punchline: Alex's practical question about who's paying, Elaine's dismissive response
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast between romantic idealism and practical concerns.

jokes.text: 'The ruins of the temple of Apollo.' 'We didn't do this.'
jokes.setup: Looking at ancient ruins for postcard
jokes.punchline: Feeling need to clarify they weren't responsible for the destruction
jokes.explanation: Clever absurdist humor about tourist guilt and postcard writing conventions.

jokes.text: Oh, Alexo, to us Greeks, there is no type. Only the sea and love, and love of the sea.
jokes.setup: Elaine suggesting Oumas isn't Alex's type
jokes.punchline: Alex's pretentious pseudo-philosophical response in fake Greek mode
jokes.explanation: Excellent character comedy showing Alex adopting pretentious persona when drunk/in foreign culture.

jokes.text: Yo! Yo! Desiree! I'm in here. I'm in here. She's coming. She's coming in. Elaine, excuse me. My date is shimmering in the doorway.
jokes.setup: Alex spotting his date arriving
jokes.punchline: His over-the-top excitement and poetic description of 'shimmering'
jokes.explanation: Good Alex moment - his romantic delusions expressed through flowery language

jokes.text: taxi drivers are held in very high esteem in America. There's a waiting list. People commit suicide if they can't get in. Now, doctors, over in America, you wouldn't want to talk to them. Riffraff.
jokes.setup: Alex embarrassed about being a taxi driver to sophisticated French woman
jokes.punchline: Increasingly absurd lies about taxi driver prestige and doctor status
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalating absurdity showing Alex's desperation and creativity when lying. The reversal of doctor/taxi driver status is inspired.

jokes.text: What kind of a woman are you? I mean, you meet a guy, and you like him, and just because he can't afford to take you to a nice place, you're gonna dump him? What kind of a person is that? French.
jokes.setup: Alex's impassioned speech about shallow materialism
jokes.punchline: Desiree's matter-of-fact 'French' as complete explanation
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/punchline with Alex's moral outrage deflated by Desiree's casual cultural explanation.

jokes.text: Why smile when we're all gonna die?
jokes.setup: Discussion of rich Viennese food
jokes.punchline: Alex's existentially depressed response to the situation
jokes.explanation: Perfect Alex character moment - philosophical depression in response to mundane situation.

jokes.text: I'll have a clear broth for now. What comes with that? A sprig of parsley. Can you substitute meatloaf?
jokes.setup: Expensive Viennese restaurant with broke Alex
jokes.punchline: Asking to substitute meatloaf for parsley garnish
jokes.explanation: Good cultural clash humor showing Alex's working-class sensibilities in upscale European setting.

jokes.text: What-what... what comes with that? A sprig of parsley. Can you substitute meatloaf?
jokes.setup: Alex ordering clear broth
jokes.punchline: His request to substitute meatloaf for parsley garnish
jokes.explanation: Great absurdist moment - the complete disconnect between fancy European cuisine and American comfort food

jokes.text: Of course. I've been waiting for you to loan me money. What'd you think I was going to say? I'd rather be hungry? Pride? Give me money.
jokes.setup: Elaine offering to loan Alex money
jokes.punchline: Alex's complete lack of pride about accepting help
jokes.explanation: Good character moment - Alex's brutal honesty about his financial situation and lack of false pride

jokes.text: And that monk who broke a 30-year vow of silence just to say wow. In Spanish!
jokes.setup: Alex lists Elaine's impressive European conquests
jokes.punchline: The most absurd example - a monk breaking silence, speaking in wrong language
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation building to an absurdist peak. The Spanish detail makes it even more ridiculous and memorable

jokes.text: I got the idea that night in Barcelona when, uh, I heard you in the next room crying yourself to sleep.
jokes.setup: Elaine asking why Alex thinks she hasn't enjoyed Europe
jokes.punchline: The revelation that she's been crying herself to sleep
jokes.explanation: Dark humor revealing the gap between European adventure fantasy and reality.

jokes.text: Oh, I don't know. Maybe I was expecting too much from Europe. I-I-I thought it was all gonna be romance, but instead, it's just all Europe.
jokes.setup: Alex reflecting on his disappointing European experience
jokes.punchline: His distinction between romantic fantasy and actual Europe
jokes.explanation: Excellent observational humor - captures the universal disappointment when reality doesn't match fantasy, perfectly delivered

jokes.text: I didn't meet that woman on a cobblestone street who doesn't speak a word of English, and without a word, makes the greatest night of my life.
jokes.setup: Alex explaining his European disappointment
jokes.punchline: His specific romantic fantasy that didn't materialize
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment showing Alex's romantic idealism and specific fantasies about European romance.

jokes.text: You solved the cube! Oh, my gosh, how did you do that? I tore off the little color spots and put them where they should be.
jokes.setup: Elaine impressed by Alex solving Rubik's cube
jokes.punchline: Alex revealing he cheated by removing and repositioning the stickers
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/punchline with Alex's practical but unimpressive solution to the puzzle.

jokes.text: 'Dear Elaine, the time we spent writing postcards together was the best part of my trip.'
jokes.setup: Alex's private postcard to Elaine
jokes.punchline: The sweet but sad admission that postcard writing was his trip highlight
jokes.explanation: Touching character moment that callbacks to the postcard writing ritual while showing Alex's emotional honesty.

jokes.text: we've seen each other depressed, neurotic, sad, angry, hostile, stupid, ugly.
jokes.setup: Elaine talking about their four-year friendship
jokes.punchline: The increasingly harsh list of negative qualities they've witnessed
jokes.explanation: Good escalating list that builds from psychological to physical flaws in realistic friendship terms.

jokes.text: I finally got a date in Europe.
jokes.setup: Alex's entire European romantic misadventure
jokes.punchline: His satisfaction at finally getting a 'date' with his friend Elaine
jokes.explanation: Perfect episode-closing callback that reframes the entire European adventure while showing character growth.
